questions.question: What directive makes a Next.js component a Client Component?
options: import 'client'
'use client'
export client = true
// @client
questions.explanation: Adding 'use client' at the top of a file marks that file and all components imported into it as Client Components that run in the browser.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of React Server Components?
options: They run faster due to JIT compilation
They fetch data and render HTML on the server, sending zero JavaScript to the client
They enable server-side event handlers
They automatically cache all database queries
questions.answer: They fetch data and render HTML on the server, sending zero JavaScript to the client
questions.explanation: Server Components render on the server, reducing client-side JavaScript bundle size, improving performance, and allowing direct database/API access without client-side fetch waterfalls.

questions.question: What does revalidatePath() do in Next.js?
options: Re-renders the component on the client
Purges the Next.js cache for a specific path, causing fresh data on the next request
Validates the URL format
Redirects to a different path
questions.answer: Purges the Next.js cache for a specific path, causing fresh data on the next request
questions.explanation: After a mutation (e.g., creating a product via a Server Action), revalidatePath('/products') tells Next.js to regenerate that page on the next visit.

questions.question: What is the benefit of using Suspense boundaries strategically in Next.js?
options: It makes all components load faster
It allows independent sections to stream in as data resolves, avoiding UI blockers
It enables client-side caching
It prevents hydration errors
questions.answer: It allows independent sections to stream in as data resolves, avoiding UI blockers
questions.explanation: Wrapping slow components in separate Suspense boundaries allows fast parts of the page to render immediately while slow parts show skeletons and stream in when their data is ready.

questions.question: What is the purpose of generateStaticParams in Next.js?
options: To validate URL parameters on the server
To pre-generate all dynamic route pages at build time
To generate mock data for testing
To create API route handlers for dynamic paths
questions.answer: To pre-generate all dynamic route pages at build time
questions.explanation: generateStaticParams returns a list of params (e.g., all blog slugs), and Next.js pre-renders a static page for each, making them extremely fast to serve.
